title: 25:28 â December 1, 2025
body_text:
25:28 â December 1, 2025
Update to the Wires Part
We updated the Wires Part to reflect changes to the Sender FI to Receiver FI Information section, as described in the following news item:
Update to Wires: Sender FI to Receiver FI Information Optional Field
. We also made other minor changes to the Part.
Electronic Transactions Volume
Wires Part
Section 16.14, Completing the Sender FI to Receiver FI Information Section
We updated this section to reflect the changes to the Sender FI to Receiver FI section from six fields to two fields for regular wires.
Section 17.9, Completing the Sender FI to Receiver FI Information Section
We updated this section to reflect the changes to the Sender FI to Receiver FI section from six fields to two fields for bank to bank wires.
Section 25.2, Initiating Trace Requests, Recalls, and Amendments for Outgoing Wires
We updated this section to reflect minor changes to the Wires Trace, Recall, and Amendment Request form in ServiceNow.
Throughout
We added the following note to relevant sections of the Part pertaining to wire traces, recalls, and amendments:
Important
: Wire recalls, amendments, and trace requests are processed on a best-efforts basis and arenât guaranteed. Once a wire has been released by Central 1, the receiving bank must voluntarily agree to return the funds. Central 1 will make every reasonable effort to support your request, however, the receiving institution may decline, delay, or be unable to return the funds.
Throughout
We updated references for ârelationship managerâ to âsenior account executiveâ and made other minor editorial changes to various sections in the Part.
Document Library
Status
Wires Trace, Recall, and Amendment Request
1917
We updated the form name and added Compliance Request and Fraud Recall Request to the request types to match the available options in ServiceNow. We also added the following note:
Wire recalls, amendments, and trace requests are processed on a best-efforts basis and arenât guaranteed. Once a wire has been released by Central 1, the receiving bank must voluntarily agree to return the funds. Central 1 will make every reasonable effort to support your request, however, the receiving institution may decline, delay, or be unable to return the funds.
